---
title: "Local Setup"
description: "Environment setup, make targets, and database tooling for running Polydelve locally."
---

## Prerequisites

- Python 3.14+, [uv](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/)
- Node.js 20+, npm
- Docker (for local Postgres)

## Install

```bash
make be-install    # backend deps via uv
make fe-install    # frontend deps via npm
```

## Environment variables

Copy the secrets template and fill in keys:

```bash
cp backend/secrets/.env.example backend/secrets/.env
```

| Variable | Service | Required for |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| `AUTH0_DOMAIN` | Auth0 | Auth (can use dev tenant) |
| `AUTH0_AUDIENCE` | Auth0 | Auth |
| `EXA_API_KEY` | Exa | News fetch |
| `GEMINI_API_KEY` | Google Gemini | News tagging, sector LLM |
| `OPENAI_API_KEY` | OpenAI | Sector classification fallback |

For local dev the app runs against a local Postgres instance — no cloud DB token needed.

## Run

```bash
make dev     # backend (port 8000) + frontend (port 5173) concurrently
make be      # backend only
make fe      # frontend only
```

## Local Postgres

`make dev` starts a Dockerized Postgres 16 instance automatically and stops it on Ctrl-C.

```
postgresql://polydelve:polydelve@127.0.0.1:5432/polydelve_dev
```

Schema is managed by Alembic. Run migrations after first clone:

```bash
cd backend && uv run alembic upgrade head
```

## Useful make targets

```
make news-update            fetch and store latest security news
make build-cve-history      pull CVE data from OSV for all packages
make enrich-packages        add download stats and descriptions
make enrich-sectors         heuristic sector classification
make classify-sectors-llm   LLM sector classification (slower)
make refresh-epss           update EPSS scores + resolve contracts
make be-test                run backend test suite
make lint                   lint frontend TypeScript
```

Run `make help` to see all targets.

## nvim-dbee

[nvim-dbee](https://github.com/kndndrj/nvim-dbee) is wired to the local connection out of the box via `~/.config/nvim/lua/plugins/dbee.lua`.

```
:Dbee   — open the UI
```

The `polydelve-local` connection is pre-configured. No env vars needed for local dev.

<img src="https://assets.polydelve.com/dbee-local.png" alt="nvim-dbee querying the local Postgres DB" />

### Key bindings

| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| `<CR>` | Execute query (`n` = next page) |
| `cw` | Close window |
| `dd` | Delete note |
| `<Esc>` / `q` | Close menu |

## Connecting to RDS (prod)

When RDS is provisioned, temporarily allowlist your IP in the RDS security group (port 5432) and add a second connection to `dbee.lua`:

```lua
{
  name = "polydelve-prod",
  type = "postgres",
  url = "postgresql://<user>:<pass>@<rds-endpoint>:5432/polydelve",
},
```

Revoke the security group rule when done.
